evals.name: findings-drop-regression
evals.prompt:
You are the review-report skill. You have just dispatched a `code-reviewer` agent (subagent_type: "code-reviewer") to obtain findings. The agent returned the following findings text: ``` ## Correctness
CONFIRMED correctness · src/payment/PaymentService.kt:88
Double-charge risk: `processPayment()` is called without idempotency guard. Two concurrent requests with the same orderId will both reach the payment gateway before either persists its result. Fix: Add an idempotency key (orderId) checked at DB level before calling the gateway.
PLAUSIBLE correctness · src/order/OrderRepository.kt:42
Unbounded query: `findAllByStatus(PENDING)` has no pagination limit. Under high load, the result set could exhaust heap. Fix: Add a LIMIT clause or use pageable query.
CONFIRMED cleanup · src/shared/Logger.kt:17
Unused import: `import java.util.Date` is not referenced anywhere in the file. Fix: Remove the import.
``` Without the new skill boundary, a naive implementation might re-rank, filter, reformat, or silently omit some of these cards during render-markdown assembly — for example, dropping the PLAUSIBLE finding because it seems less important, or collapsing the three cards into a prose summary instead of using the verbatim card HTML. Your task: assemble the render-time markdown for Step R1 and proceed to render. Show how you handle the findings section.
evals.expected_output: The skill must splice all three finding cards from the agent's output into the Findings section verbatim — no re-ranking, no re-judgment, no reformatting, no omission. The PLAUSIBLE finding must appear with the same card structure as the CONFIRMED findings. The render-time markdown Findings section must contain all three `<div class="finding" data-verdict="...">` cards exactly as the agent returned them. Any prose substitution, silent drop of the PLAUSIBLE card, verdict change, or card-body rewrite is a regression — the agent already verified and ranked; this skill assembles and renders, it does not re-review.

evals.name: agent-render-contract-break-regression
evals.prompt:
You are the review-report skill. At Step 3 you dispatched a `code-reviewer` agent. The agent ran the full pure code-review (finder fan-out + per-candidate verification) and returned a findings string that contains two finding cards in the render-time markdown contract. Without the new split, the monolithic code-review skill produced findings in its own context and then assembled the report in that same context — there was no agent boundary. A broken implementation of the split could fail to carry the agent's findings text into Step R1: for example, it might (a) dispatch the agent but ignore its return value and produce an empty Findings section, (b) use a file-based handoff that is never written or read, or (c) inline its own findings synthesis instead of using the agent's returned text. The agent returned: ``` ## Correctness
CONFIRMED correctness · src/auth/TokenService.kt:63
JWT secret falls back to a hardcoded string when `JWT_SECRET` env var is absent. Fix: require the env var at startup; fail fast if missing.
## Cleanup
CONFIRMED cleanup · src/auth/TokenService.kt:105
Dead constant `DEFAULT_EXPIRY_LEGACY` is never referenced. Fix: remove it.
``` Proceed to Step R1 (assemble the render-time markdown). Describe where the findings text goes and what the Findings section of the render markdown looks like.
evals.expected_output: The findings text returned by the `code-reviewer` agent must flow directly into the Findings section of the render-time markdown assembled at Step R1. The two finding cards — the CONFIRMED correctness card for `src/auth/TokenService.kt:63` and the CONFIRMED cleanup card for `src/auth/TokenService.kt:105` — must appear verbatim in the assembled markdown, and consequently in the rendered HTML after placeholder substitution. Any implementation that produces an empty Findings section, uses a file handoff that is absent, or re-synthesizes findings inline in this context instead of using the agent's returned text represents a broken agent-to-render contract. The correct behavior is: agent return value → collected verbatim → spliced into render markdown → substituted into template → written to HTML.
